output.final_deliverable:
I visited https://github.com/python/cpython (visit reported as successful). The page content returned in the merged results is: "https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer" and the visit returned 20 page links (sample: https://github.com/, https://github.com/login?return_to=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fpython%2Fcpython, https://github.com/features/copilot). Based only on the provided evidence, the requested repository metadata could not be extracted. Specifically:

- Primary programming language: MISSING (not present in the provided page content)
- Number of stars (approximate): MISSING (not present in the provided page content)
- License type: MISSING (not present in the provided page content)
- Brief repository description: MISSING (not present in the provided page content)

Evidence / citations:
- Visited URL: https://github.com/python/cpython
- Returned content snippet: "https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer" (from the visit result)

Because the visit returned the support page snippet rather than the repository HTML/text, the metadata requested is not available in the merged results.
output.action_summary: Attempted to visit https://github.com/python/cpython; the merged results show the visit succeeded but returned a support page snippet ("https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer") and a set of links. The repository metadata (primary language, star count, license, description) is not present in the provided content, so those items are missing from the evidence.

result.title: Assemble, validate, and format extracted repository metadata

details.intent: Take the raw extraction results returned by the visit node, validate each target_fact for plausibility, normalize formats (e.g., convert '53k' to approx integer), and produce a final structured JSON output of the four requested items plus evidence and source.

validation_checks.primary_language: must be a non-empty short string; if multiple languages listed, choose the highest percentage or first listed
validation_checks.stars_count: must parse to integer; if abbreviated with suffix (k, M), convert to numeric approximation and set 'approx' flag
validation_checks.license_type: prefer explicit license label; if only LICENSE filename present, read first line or header snippet for license name; otherwise 'Not found'
validation_checks.brief_description: extract the visible repo description; trim to one sentence or 240 chars

details.reasoning: This step consolidates raw page data into normalized, verifiable outputs. It ensures the numbers and labels are interpretable and that any approximations are flagged for transparency.

1f0a357f-3289-49a5-b921-f5354b3b6e18.title: Visit the GitHub repository page for python/cpython and extract metadata

details.intent: Directly retrieve the repository page HTML/text to extract: (1) primary programming language, (2) number of stars (approximate), (3) license type, (4) brief repository description. Must rely on this page only (no search results).

target_facts.stars_count: integer (approximate; if formatted like '53k', return numeric approx e.g., 53000)
target_facts.brief_description: one-sentence string summarizing the repository description as shown on the page

details.extraction_plan: From the page content, locate the repository header/metadata area. Extract: (a) primary language from the language bar or language label (often shown near top-right or in repo language stats), (b) star count from the 'Star' button / social counts shown near the top, (c) license from the license badge/label in the repo metadata or link to LICENSE file in repository summary, (d) description from the repository title/description area beneath the repo name. Capture the exact text snippets for each fact and include the HTML snippet or surrounding text as evidence.

measurable_output_fields.stars_count: integer (>=0) and an 'approx' flag if rounded)
measurable_output_fields.evidence_snippets: raw text/HTML snippets showing where each fact was taken from

details.verification_steps: 1) Ensure the star count appears in the page near the 'Star' control; if abbreviated (e.g., '54k'), convert to approximate integer. 2) Confirm license by looking for explicit 'License' label or a link to a LICENSE file; capture both the label and link text. 3) Confirm primary language by locating the language bar or language list and selecting the highest-percentage language or single primary label. 4) If any field is missing, mark it 'Not found' and include the page snippet context.
details.rationale: Visiting the repository page directly is the authoritative source for the requested metadata. This reduces uncertainty by extracting facts as they are presented on GitHub and capturing evidence snippets for verification.
details.parent_goal: Visit the GitHub repository at https://github.com/python/cpython and extract the following information: (1) The primary programming language used, (2) The number of stars (approximate), (3) The license type, (4) A brief description of what the repository is. You must visit the GitHub page directly - do not rely on search results.
